# Implement Plan

You are tasked with implementing an approved technical plan from `thoughts/shared/plans/`. These plans contain phases with specific changes and success criteria.

## Getting Started

When given a plan path:

- Read the plan completely and check for any existing checkmarks (- [x])
- Read the original ticket and all files mentioned in the plan
- **Read files fully** - never use limit/offset parameters, you need complete context
- Think deeply about how the pieces fit together
- Create a todo list to track your progress
- Start implementing if you understand what needs to be done

If no plan path provided, ask for one.

## Autonomous Mode

When invoked with `autonomous: true` in the parameters:

- Do NOT pause for manual verification between phases
- Implement exactly ONE phase, then exit
- Run only automated verification (skip manual verification steps entirely)
- Do NOT use AskUserQuestion at any point
- If automated verification passes, mark the phase done:
  1. Check off automated verification items (`- [x]`)
  2. **Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading** (e.g., `## Phase 1: Title` → `## Phase 1: Title [DONE]`). This is critical — the loop uses this marker to detect phase completion.
  3. Commit and exit
- If stuck after 3 fix attempts, exit and note the issue in the plan file

## Implementation Philosophy

Plans are carefully designed, but reality can be messy. Your job is to:

- Follow the plan's intent while adapting to what you find
- Implement each phase fully before moving to the next
- Verify your work makes sense in the broader codebase context
- Update checkboxes in the plan as you complete sections

When things don't match the plan exactly, think about why and communicate clearly. The plan is your guide, but your judgment matters too.

If you encounter a mismatch:

- STOP and think deeply about why the plan can't be followed
- Present the issue clearly:

  ```
  Issue in Phase [N]:
  Expected: [what the plan says]
  Found: [actual situation]
  Why this matters: [explanation]

  How should I proceed?
  ```

## Verification Approach

After implementing a phase:

- Run the success criteria checks
- Fix any issues before proceeding
- Update your progress in both the plan and your todos
- Check off completed items in the plan file itself using Edit
- **In autonomous mode**: skip the pause below. Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading, commit, and exit.
- **Pause for human verification** (interactive mode only): After completing all automated verification for a phase, pause and inform the human that the phase is ready for manual testing. Use this format:

  ```
  Phase [N] Complete - Ready for Manual Verification

  Automated verification passed:
  - [List automated checks that passed]

  Please perform the manual verification steps listed in the plan:
  - [List manual verification items from the plan]

  Let me know when manual testing is complete so I can proceed to Phase [N+1].
  ```

If instructed to execute multiple phases consecutively, skip the pause until the last phase. Otherwise, assume you are just doing one phase.

Do not check off items in the manual testing steps until confirmed by the user.

## If You Get Stuck

When something isn't working as expected:

- First, make sure you've read and understood all the relevant code
- Consider if the codebase has evolved since the plan was written
- Present the mismatch clearly and ask for guidance

Use sub-tasks sparingly - mainly for targeted debugging or exploring unfamiliar territory.

## Resuming Work

If the plan has existing checkmarks:

- Trust that completed work is done
- Pick up from the first unchecked item
- Verify previous work only if something seems off

Remember: You're implementing a solution, not just checking boxes. Keep the end goal in mind and maintain forward momentum.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context

1	# Marketing Integrations Page Implementation Plan
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Ship a marketing `/integrations` index and `/integrations/<id>` detail pages on `apps/www`, modelled on Linear's integrations surface. Reuses the existing fumadocs-mdx content pipeline (same pattern as blog/changelog/legal). GitHub is the only detail page authored in v1; Vercel and Linear appear as cards on the index; Vercel/Linear/Sentry/Apollo detail MDX land in follow-up PRs. Adds a top-level `Integrations` entry to `INTERNAL_NAV`.
6	
7	## Current State Analysis
8	
9	- `apps/www` has four existing MDX collections — `docs`, `apiDocs`, `blogCollection`, `changelogCollection`, `legalCollection` — all defined in `apps/www/source.config.ts:13-39` with Zod schemas in `apps/www/src/lib/content-schemas.ts`.
10	- The fumadocs loader is wrapped in `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/_lib/source.ts:97-105` exposing `getChangelogPage/getChangelogPages` helpers — the direct template for integrations.
11	- JSON-LD builders live per-content-type in `apps/www/src/lib/builders/` and are re-exported from `apps/www/src/lib/builders/index.ts`.
12	- SEO bundles in `apps/www/src/lib/seo-bundle.ts` fuse metadata + JSON-LD into typed `SeoBundle` returns per content type.
13	- URL-type brands live in `apps/www/src/lib/url-types.ts`.
14	- Nav is defined in `apps/www/src/config/nav.ts` with compile-time `Route<H>` validation via `defineNavItems` in `apps/www/src/types/nav.ts`.
15	- `apps/www/src/app/sitemap.ts` is the authoritative sitemap for the www zone (research doc incorrectly attributed it to `apps/app`).
16	- Provider display metadata lives in `packages/app-providers/src/client/display.ts` (`PROVIDER_DISPLAY`, `providerSlugSchema` enum with `apollo`/`github`/`vercel`/`linear`/`sentry`).
17	- Broader icon set (14 providers) in `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx` (`IntegrationLogoIcons`).
18	- `<IntegrationShowcase>` already in use on the landing page at `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/integration-showcase.tsx`.
19	- `@repo/og` `<ContentLayout>` (packages/og/src/layouts/content.tsx) is the canonical OG image layout — reused by blog/changelog.
20	- Existing connector docs at `apps/www/src/content/docs/connectors/{github,linear,vercel,sentry}.mdx` (technical surface; cross-linked but separate from marketing).
21	- No `/integrations` route exists today.
22	
23	### Key Discoveries
24	
25	- `ProviderSlug` enum (`packages/app-providers/src/client/display.ts:15-23`) is client-safe and can be imported into `apps/www/src/lib/content-schemas.ts` without pulling server-only provider code.
26	- `createArticleMetadata` (`apps/www/src/lib/content-seo.ts:37-55`) forces `og:type: "article"` — we want `createMetadata` with `og:type: "website"` for integrations (matches legal pages pattern).
27	- Canonical URL pattern in schemas uses `.refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/<prefix>/"))` per content type — same pattern applies to integrations.
28	- `mdxComponents` spread `defaultMdxComponents` first then overrides — new components (`IntegrationHero`, etc.) slot in at the component map.
29	- Sitemap already sorts blog/changelog entries by `publishedAt`; integrations have no `publishedAt` so we emit them with static priority and no `lastModified` (or use a file-mtime-free approach).
30	
31	## Desired End State
32	
33	- `/integrations` renders a static index listing GitHub, Vercel, and Linear, with coming-soon badges driven by `PROVIDER_DISPLAY.comingSoon` (override via MDX `status`).
34	- `/integrations/github` renders a marketing detail page with hero, feature grid, screenshots, FAQ, CTAs. OG image served at `/integrations/github/opengraph-image`.
35	- `Integrations` appears as a top-level nav item on desktop, mobile, and pitch-deck navbars (all three auto-pick-up from `INTERNAL_NAV`).
36	- Sitemap emits `/integrations` and `/integrations/github` with proper priorities.
37	- JSON-LD includes `Organization`, `WebSite`, `WebPage` (the marketing page), `SoftwareApplication` (the provider being integrated), `BreadcrumbList`, and `FAQPage` when FAQ present.
38	- PostHog events fire with `providerId` on both index-card clicks and detail CTA clicks.
39	- `pnpm typecheck --filter=@repo/www` passes; `pnpm check --filter=@repo/www` passes; `pnpm build --filter=@repo/www` passes.
40	
41	### Verification
42	
43	Load `http://localhost:4101/integrations` in a browser: index grid renders with three cards, Linear shows coming-soon badge. Click GitHub card → lands on `/integrations/github` with hero, features, FAQ. View page source: JSON-LD `@graph` contains the expected entities. `curl http://localhost:4101/sitemap.xml | grep integrations` returns both URLs.
44	
45	## What We're NOT Doing
46	
47	- RSS/Atom/feed routes (integrations are not a news stream).
48	- Prev/next navigation between integrations.
49	- Auto-generating pages from the `PROVIDERS` registry (marketing needs human-authored differentiating copy).
50	- Per-integration waitlist forms (reuse generic `WaitlistCTA`).
51	- Vercel, Linear, Sentry, Apollo detail MDX (follow-up PRs after pattern is validated).
52	- Fumadocs in-app search indexing (parity with blog/changelog — not a regression).
53	- Remotion programmatic featured-image templates (follow-up).
54	- Unified merge with `/docs/connectors/*` (dual-surface strategy is intentional per research).
55	- Generic content-type factory abstractions (clone the changelog pipeline inline).
56	
57	## Implementation Approach
58	
59	Clone the changelog pipeline pattern near-verbatim, strip chronology (no `publishedAt`, no feeds, no prev/next sort), and add integration-specific surface (hero/feature grid components, optional `providerId` linkage to `PROVIDER_DISPLAY`). Build the schema + data pipeline first, then SEO infrastructure, then components, then pages, then wiring. GitHub MDX proves the end-to-end pattern before Vercel/Linear MDX land as content-only PRs.
60	
61	---
62	
63	## Phase 1: Content Pipeline Foundation
64	
65	### Overview
66	
67	Introduce the `IntegrationPageSchema`, register `integrationsCollection`, wrap with fumadocs loader, add typed URL brand, and scaffold the content directory.
68	
69	### Changes Required
70	
71	#### 1. Schema — `apps/www/src/lib/content-schemas.ts`
72	
73	Add `IntegrationPageSchema` extending `BasePageSchema` directly (NOT `ContentPageSchema` — `authors`/`publishedAt` don't apply). Reuse existing `FaqItemSchema` (already defined). Import `providerSlugSchema` from `@repo/app-providers` client bundle.
74	
75	```ts
76	import { providerSlugSchema } from "@repo/app-providers/client";
77	
78	const IntegrationStatusSchema = z.enum(["live", "beta", "coming-soon"]);
79	const IntegrationCategorySchema = z.enum([
80	  "dev-tools",
81	  "monitoring",
82	  "comms",
83	  "data",
84	  "project-management",
85	]);
86	
87	export const IntegrationPageSchema = BasePageSchema.extend({
88	  canonicalUrl: z
89	    .url()
90	    .refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/integrations/"))
91	    .optional(),
92	  providerId: providerSlugSchema.optional(),
93	  tagline: z.string().min(10).max(120),
94	  category: IntegrationCategorySchema,
95	  featuredImage: z.string().startsWith("/images/").optional(),
96	  docsUrl: z.string().startsWith("/docs/").optional(),
97	  status: IntegrationStatusSchema.optional(),
98	  faq: z.array(FaqItemSchema).min(1).optional(),
99	  updatedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
100	});
101	
102	export type IntegrationPageData = z.infer<typeof IntegrationPageSchema>;
103	export type IntegrationCategory = IntegrationPageData["category"];
104	export type IntegrationStatus = IntegrationPageData["status"];
105	```
106	
107	Verify `@repo/app-providers/client` export path exists — search for `client/display` re-export in package `index.ts` or `package.json` exports map. If `providerSlugSchema` isn't client-surfaced, add it to the package's client barrel.
108	
109	#### 2. Collection registration — `apps/www/source.config.ts`
110	
111	Add collection after `legalCollection`:
112	
113	```ts
114	import {
115	  BlogPostSchema,
116	  ChangelogEntrySchema,
117	  DocsPageSchema,
118	  IntegrationPageSchema,
119	  LegalPageSchema,
120	} from "./src/lib/content-schemas";
121	
122	export const integrationsCollection = defineCollections({
123	  type: "doc",
124	  dir: "src/content/integrations",
125	  schema: IntegrationPageSchema,
126	});
127	```
128	
129	#### 3. Source loader — `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/_lib/source.ts`
130	
131	Add after the legal section:
132	
133	```ts
134	import {
135	  apiDocs,
136	  apiMeta,
137	  blogCollection,
138	  changelogCollection,
139	  docs,
140	  integrationsCollection,
141	  legalCollection,
142	  meta,
143	} from "fumadocs-mdx:collections/server";
144	
145	// ... existing code ...
146	
147	// --- Integrations ---
148	const integrationsSource = loader({
149	  baseUrl: "/integrations",
150	  source: toFumadocsSource(integrationsCollection, []),
151	});
152	
153	export const getIntegrationPage = (slugs: string[]) =>
154	  integrationsSource.getPage(slugs);
155	export const getIntegrationPages = () => integrationsSource.getPages();
156	```
157	
158	#### 4. URL brand — `apps/www/src/lib/url-types.ts`
159	
160	```ts
161	export type IntegrationUrl = LightfastUrl<`integrations/${string}`>;
162	```
163	
164	#### 5. Content directory — `apps/www/src/content/integrations/`
165	
166	Create the directory (empty). A placeholder `.gitkeep` is unnecessary because GitHub MDX lands in Phase 5.
167	
168	### Success Criteria
169	
170	#### Automated Verification
171	
172	- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter=@repo/www` passes
173	- [ ] `pnpm check --filter=@repo/www` passes
174	- [ ] `pnpm build --filter=@repo/www` succeeds (fumadocs-mdx generates types for empty `integrationsCollection`)
175	
176	#### Manual Verification
177	
178	- [ ] Importing `getIntegrationPages` from source.ts in a scratch file type-checks against expected shape
179	- [ ] `providerSlugSchema` is importable client-side (no server imports pulled in)
180	
181	---
182	
183	## Phase 2: SEO Infrastructure
184	
185	### Overview
186	
187	Build the JSON-LD graph builder, add `emitIntegrationSeo` to the seo-bundle, and wire re-exports.
188	
189	### Changes Required
190	
191	#### 1. JSON-LD builder — `apps/www/src/lib/builders/integrations.ts` (new file)
192	
193	```ts
194	import type {
195	  FAQPage,
196	  GraphContext,
197	  SoftwareApplication,
198	  WebPage,
199	} from "@vendor/seo/json-ld";
200	import { PROVIDER_DISPLAY } from "@repo/app-providers/client";
201	import type { IntegrationPageData } from "~/lib/content-schemas";
202	import type { IntegrationUrl } from "~/lib/url-types";
203	import {
204	  buildBreadcrumbList,
205	  buildFaqEntity,
206	  buildOrganizationEntity,
207	  buildWebSiteEntity,
208	} from "./shared";
209	
210	function buildIntegrationPageEntity(
211	  data: IntegrationPageData,
212	  url: IntegrationUrl
213	): Omit<WebPage, "@id" | "url"> {
214	  return {
215	    "@type": "WebPage",
216	    name: data.title,
217	    description: data.description,
218	    dateModified: data.updatedAt,
219	    inLanguage: "en-US",
220	    isPartOf: { "@id": "https://lightfast.ai/#website" },
221	    publisher: { "@id": "https://lightfast.ai/#organization" },
222	    about: data.providerId
223	      ? { "@id": `${url}#integrated-app` }
224	      : undefined,
225	    keywords: data.keywords.join(", "),
226	  };
227	}
228	
229	function buildIntegratedAppEntity(
230	  data: IntegrationPageData,
231	  url: IntegrationUrl
232	): SoftwareApplication | null {
233	  if (!data.providerId) return null;
234	  const display = PROVIDER_DISPLAY[data.providerId];
235	  return {
236	    "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
237	    "@id": `${url}#integrated-app`,
238	    name: display.displayName,
239	    description: display.description,
240	    applicationCategory: "DeveloperApplication",
241	    operatingSystem: "Web",
242	  };
243	}
244	
245	export function buildIntegrationJsonLd(
246	  data: IntegrationPageData,
247	  url: IntegrationUrl
248	): GraphContext {
249	  const entity = buildIntegrationPageEntity(data, url);
250	  const integratedApp = buildIntegratedAppEntity(data, url);
251	  return {
252	    "@context": "https://schema.org",
253	    "@graph": [
254	      buildOrganizationEntity(),
255	      buildWebSiteEntity(),
256	      { ...entity, "@id": `${url}#webpage`, url },
257	      ...(integratedApp ? [integratedApp] : []),
258	      ...(data.faq && data.faq.length > 0 ? [buildFaqEntity(data.faq, url)] : []),
259	      buildBreadcrumbList([
260	        { name: "Home", url: "https://lightfast.ai" },
261	        { name: "Integrations", url: "https://lightfast.ai/integrations" },
262	        { name: data.title, url },
263	      ]),
264	    ],
265	  };
266	}
267	```
268	
269	Verify `SoftwareApplication` type is exported from `@vendor/seo/json-ld`. If not, either add it to the vendor barrel or use the structural shape directly (following the same pattern the changelog builder uses for `BlogPosting`).
270	
271	#### 2. Builder re-export — `apps/www/src/lib/builders/index.ts`
272	
273	```ts
274	export { buildIntegrationJsonLd } from "./integrations";
275	```
276	
277	#### 3. SEO bundle — `apps/www/src/lib/seo-bundle.ts`
278	
279	Add alongside existing emitters:
280	
281	```ts
282	import type { IntegrationPageData } from "./content-schemas";
283	import type { IntegrationUrl } from "./url-types";
284	import { buildIntegrationJsonLd } from "./builders";
285	
286	export function emitIntegrationSeo(
287	  data: IntegrationPageData,
288	  url: IntegrationUrl
289	): SeoBundle {
290	  const canonicalUrl = data.canonicalUrl ?? url;
291	  const ogImageUrl = `${url}/opengraph-image`;
292	  return {
293	    metadata: createMetadata({
294	      title: `${data.title} – Lightfast Integrations`,
295	      description: data.description,
296	      keywords: data.keywords,
297	      creator: "Lightfast",
298	      publisher: "Lightfast",
299	      robots: {
300	        index: !data.noindex,
301	        follow: !data.nofollow,
302	        googleBot: {
303	          index: !data.noindex,
304	          follow: !data.nofollow,
305	          "max-video-preview": -1,
306	          "max-image-preview": "large",
307	          "max-snippet": -1,
308	        },
309	      },
310	      alternates: { canonical: canonicalUrl },
311	      openGraph: {
312	        title: data.ogTitle,
313	        description: data.ogDescription,
314	        type: "website",
315	        url: canonicalUrl,
316	        siteName: "Lightfast Integrations",
317	        locale: "en_US",
318	        images: [{ url: ogImageUrl, width: 1200, height: 630, alt: data.ogTitle }],
319	      },
320	      twitter: {
321	        card: "summary_large_image",
322	        title: data.ogTitle,
323	        description: data.ogDescription,
324	        site: "@lightfastai",
325	        creator: "@lightfastai",
326	        images: [ogImageUrl],
327	      },
328	    }),
329	    jsonLd: buildIntegrationJsonLd(data, url),
330	  };
331	}
332	```
333	
334	### Success Criteria
335	
336	#### Automated Verification
337	
338	- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter=@repo/www` passes
339	- [ ] `pnpm check --filter=@repo/www` passes
340	
341	#### Manual Verification
342	
343	- [ ] Calling `emitIntegrationSeo` in a scratch file returns the expected `{ metadata, jsonLd }` shape
344	- [ ] `jsonLd["@graph"]` includes `Organization`, `WebSite`, `WebPage`, `SoftwareApplication`, `BreadcrumbList`
345	
346	---
347	
348	## Phase 3: MDX Components & Provider Helpers
349	
350	### Overview
351	
352	Add `getProviderIcon(providerId)` helper, and three new MDX components — `IntegrationHero`, `IntegrationFeatureGrid`, `IntegrationScreenshot` — wired into `mdxComponents`.
353	
354	### Changes Required
355	
356	#### 1. Provider icon helper — `apps/www/src/lib/get-provider-icon.ts` (new file)
357	
358	```ts
359	import { IntegrationLogoIcons } from "@repo/ui/integration-icons";
360	import type { ProviderSlug } from "@repo/app-providers/client";
361	
362	const PROVIDER_TO_ICON_KEY: Record<ProviderSlug, keyof typeof IntegrationLogoIcons> = {
363	  apollo: "apollo",
364	  github: "github",
365	  vercel: "vercel",
366	  linear: "linear",
367	  sentry: "sentry",
368	};
369	
370	export function getProviderIcon(providerId: ProviderSlug) {
371	  return IntegrationLogoIcons[PROVIDER_TO_ICON_KEY[providerId]];
372	}
373	```
374

## Why connect GitHub?

Your repos are the source of truth for what your team shipped, reviewed, and deployed. Connecting GitHub turns that activity into queryable knowledge — ask "what did Alex ship last sprint?" and Lightfast answers from real PR data.

## How it works

1. Click **Install GitHub** on the Lightfast integrations settings page.
2. Authorize the Lightfast GitHub App on the repos you want to connect.
3. Events start flowing immediately. Backfill runs in the background.
4. Query your engineering activity through chat or the API.

<NextLink href="/docs/connectors/github">Read the full GitHub connector docs →</NextLink>

    Read-only access to repositories, issues, pull requests, and workflow runs. Lightfast never writes to your repos.
  </FAQItem>
  <FAQItem value="backfill" question="How long does backfill take?">
    Historical events backfill in minutes for small repos, up to an hour for large orgs. Progress is visible in your workspace.
  </FAQItem>
</FAQAccordion>

7. Open `/sitemap.xml` → verify 4 integration URLs present.
8. Resize browser to 375px, 768px, 1200px → verify responsive layout on both index and detail.
9. Verify PostHog panel shows `integration_card_clicked` events after clicking cards (if PostHog is wired — otherwise a known gap documented).
10. Verify desktop and mobile nav both show `Integrations` top-level entry.
11. `pnpm build --filter=@repo/www` → verify static generation succeeds and emits `/integrations/*.html` files.

## Performance Considerations

- All pages use `dynamic = "force-static"` — no runtime cost beyond static asset serving.
- OG images generated at build time via `next/og` `ImageResponse` — fonts loaded from `~/lib/og-fonts`, matching the existing cached pattern.
- MDX compiled at build time by fumadocs-mdx — zero runtime compilation cost.
- PostHog events are client-side only; no server impact.

## Migration Notes

No data migration. Purely additive — new routes, new collection, new nav entry. No existing URL or behavior changes.

## Follow-up Work (explicitly out of scope)

- Vercel full marketing MDX body (screenshots, feature narrative).
- Linear full marketing MDX body (post-GA or in beta).
- Sentry, Apollo integration pages.
- Additional integration cards from the 14-logo icon set (Slack, Notion, PostHog, Datadog, etc.) — requires backend work before marketing surface.
- Remotion programmatic featured-image generator per integration.
- Per-integration waitlist forms with Resend or similar.
- Fumadocs search integration for integrations collection.
- Index filtering by category.
- Animated hero backgrounds or interactive demos per integration.

## References

- Research doc: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-14-marketing-integrations-page.md`
- AEO guidance: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-10-changelog-aeo-best-practices.md`
- Changelog pipeline (template): `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/changelog/`
- Schema source: `apps/www/src/lib/content-schemas.ts:21-38`
- Loader pattern: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/_lib/source.ts:97-105`
- SEO bundle: `apps/www/src/lib/seo-bundle.ts:112-126`
- Nav config: `apps/www/src/config/nav.ts:10-14`
- Sitemap: `apps/www/src/app/sitemap.ts`
- Provider display: `packages/app-providers/src/client/display.ts:15-90`
- OG layout: `packages/og/src/layouts/content.tsx`

13	});

16	  name: z.string().min(1),

18	  url: z.url().optional(),
19	});
20	
21	const BasePageSchema = z.object({
22	  title: z.string().min(1),
23	  description: z.string().min(50).max(160),
24	  keywords: z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(3).max(20),
25	  canonicalUrl: z.url(),
26	  ogTitle: z.string().min(1).max(70),
27	  ogDescription: z.string().min(50).max(160),
28	  noindex: z.boolean().default(false),
29	  nofollow: z.boolean().default(false),
30	});
31	
32	const ContentPageSchema = BasePageSchema.extend({
33	  authors: z.array(AuthorSchema).min(1),
34	  publishedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
35	  updatedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
36	  faq: z.array(FaqItemSchema).min(1),
37	  featuredImage: z.string().startsWith("/images/").optional(),
38	});
39	
40	export const BlogPostSchema = ContentPageSchema.extend({
41	  canonicalUrl: z
42	    .url()
43	    .refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/blog/"))
44	    .optional(),
45	  category: z.enum([
46	    "engineering",
47	    "product",
48	    "company",
49	    "tutorial",
50	    "research",
51	  ]),
52	  readingTimeMinutes: z.number().int().min(1),
53	  featured: z.boolean().default(false),
54	  tldr: z.string().min(20).max(300),
55	  howToSteps: z.array(HowToStepSchema).min(2).optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export const ChangelogEntrySchema = ContentPageSchema.extend({
59	  canonicalUrl: z
60	    .url()
61	    .refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/changelog/"))
62	    .optional(),
63	  version: z.string().min(1),
64	  type: z.enum(["feature", "improvement", "fix", "breaking"]),
65	  tldr: z.string().min(20).max(300),
66	  improvements: z.array(z.string().min(1)).optional(),
67	});
68	
69	export const LegalPageSchema = BasePageSchema.extend({
70	  canonicalUrl: z
71	    .url()
72	    .refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/legal/"))
73	    .optional(),
74	  updatedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
75	  effectiveAt: z.iso.datetime(),
76	});
77	
78	export const DocsPageSchema = BasePageSchema.extend({
79	  canonicalUrl: z
80	    .url()
81	    .refine((val) => val.startsWith("https://lightfast.ai/docs/"))
82	    .optional(),
83	  authors: z.array(AuthorSchema).min(1),
84	  publishedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
85	  updatedAt: z.iso.datetime(),
86	  proficiencyLevel: z
87	    .enum(["Beginner", "Intermediate", "Advanced", "Expert"])
88	    .optional(),
89	});
90	
91	// Inferred TypeScript types
92	export type BlogPostData = z.infer<typeof BlogPostSchema>;
93	export type ChangelogEntryData = z.infer<typeof ChangelogEntrySchema>;
94	export type LegalPageData = z.infer<typeof LegalPageSchema>;
95	export type DocsPageData = z.infer<typeof DocsPageSchema>;
96	
97	// Fields required by the SEO layer — satisfied structurally by BlogPostData,
98	// ChangelogEntryData, and DocsPageData. Derived from BlogPostData so schema
99	// renames propagate here automatically.
100	export type ContentSeoData = Pick<
101	  BlogPostData,
102	  | "authors"
103	  | "description"
104	  | "keywords"
105	  | "nofollow"
106	  | "noindex"
107	  | "ogDescription"
108	  | "ogTitle"
109	  | "publishedAt"
110	  | "title"
111	  | "updatedAt"
112	>;
113	
114	// Derived from Zod — Zod is the sole source of truth for these unions
115	export type BlogCategory = BlogPostData["category"];
116	// → "engineering" | "product" | "company" | "tutorial" | "research"
117	
118	export type ChangelogType = ChangelogEntryData["type"];
119	// → "feature" | "improvement" | "fix" | "breaking"

7	  buildOrganizationEntity,
8	  buildWebSiteEntity,
9	} from "./shared";
10	
11	// Exhaustive mapping — adding a new ChangelogType errors here until handled
12	const CHANGELOG_SECTIONS: Record<ChangelogType, string> = {
13	  feature: "Changelog / Feature",
14	  improvement: "Changelog / Improvement",
15	  fix: "Changelog / Fix",
16	  breaking: "Changelog / Breaking Change",
17	};

19	function buildChangelogEntryEntity(
20	  data: ChangelogEntryData,
21	  url: ChangelogUrl
22	): Omit<BlogPosting, "@id" | "url"> {
23	  return {
24	    "@type": "BlogPosting",
25	    headline: data.title,
26	    description: data.description,
27	    abstract: data.tldr,
28	    datePublished: data.publishedAt,
29	    dateModified: data.updatedAt,
30	    author: data.authors.map((a) => ({
31	      "@type": "Person" as const,
32	      name: a.name,
33	      url: a.url,
34	      ...(a.jobTitle ? { jobTitle: a.jobTitle } : {}),
35	      sameAs: [`https://x.com/${a.twitterHandle.replace(/^@/, "")}`],
36	    })),
37	    image: {
38	      "@type": "ImageObject" as const,
39	      url: `${url}/opengraph-image`,
40	      width: "1200",
41	      height: "630",
42	    },
43	    keywords: [...data.keywords, data.version, data.type].join(", "),
44	    articleSection: CHANGELOG_SECTIONS[data.type],
45	    inLanguage: "en-US",
46	    isPartOf: { "@id": "https://lightfast.ai/#website" },
47	    publisher: { "@id": "https://lightfast.ai/#organization" },
48	  };
49	}
50	
51	export function buildChangelogEntryJsonLd(
52	  data: ChangelogEntryData,
53	  url: ChangelogUrl
54	): GraphContext {
55	  const entity = buildChangelogEntryEntity(data, url);
56	  return {
57	    "@context": "https://schema.org",
58	    "@graph": [
59	      buildOrganizationEntity(),
60	      buildWebSiteEntity(),
61	      { ...entity, "@id": `${url}#article`, url },
62	      ...(data.faq.length > 0 ? [buildFaqEntity(data.faq, url)] : []),
63	      buildBreadcrumbList([
64	        { name: "Home", url: "https://lightfast.ai" },
65	        { name: "Changelog", url: "https://lightfast.ai/changelog" },
66	        { name: data.title, url },
67	      ]),
68	    ],
69	  };
70	}

1	import type { GraphContext } from "@vendor/seo/json-ld";

27	interface SeoBundle {
28	  readonly jsonLd: GraphContext;
29	  readonly metadata: Metadata;
30	}
31	
32	// SeoBundle owns canonical URL derivation — derives from the typed url param,
33	// respects frontmatter canonicalUrl as an escape hatch.
34	// Both metadata.alternates.canonical and JSON-LD url come from the same value:
35	// drift is structurally impossible.
36	
37	function buildArticleMetadata(
38	  data: ContentSeoData,
39	  canonicalUrl: string,
40	  titleSuffix: string,
41	  siteName: string,
42	  ogImageUrl?: string
43	): Metadata {
44	  return createArticleMetadata(
45	    {
46	      title: `${data.title} – ${titleSuffix}`,
47	      description: data.description,
48	      keywords: data.keywords,
49	      authors: data.authors.map((a) => ({ name: a.name, url: a.url })),
50	      creator: "Lightfast",
51	      publisher: "Lightfast",
52	      robots: {
53	        index: !data.noindex,
54	        follow: !data.nofollow,
55	        googleBot: {
56	          index: !data.noindex,
57	          follow: !data.nofollow,
58	          "max-video-preview": -1,
59	          "max-image-preview": "large",
60	          "max-snippet": -1,
61	        },
62	      },
63	      alternates: { canonical: canonicalUrl },
64	      openGraph: {
65	        title: data.ogTitle,
66	        description: data.ogDescription,
67	        url: canonicalUrl,
68	        siteName,
69	        locale: "en_US",
70	        authors: data.authors.map((a) => a.url),
71	        ...(ogImageUrl && {
72	          images: [
73	            {
74	              url: ogImageUrl,
75	              width: 1200,
76	              height: 630,
77	              alt: data.ogTitle,
78	            },
79	          ],
80	        }),
81	      },
82	      twitter: {
83	        card: "summary_large_image",
84	        title: data.ogTitle,
85	        description: data.ogDescription,
86	        site: "@lightfastai",
87	        creator: "@lightfastai",
88	        ...(ogImageUrl && { images: [ogImageUrl] }),
89	      },
90	      category: "Technology",
91	    },
92	    { publishedAt: data.publishedAt, updatedAt: data.updatedAt }
93	  );
94	}
95	
96	export function emitBlogPostSeo(
97	  data: BlogPostData,
98	  url: BlogPostUrl
99	): SeoBundle {
100	  const canonicalUrl = data.canonicalUrl ?? url;
101	  return {
102	    metadata: buildArticleMetadata(
103	      data,
104	      canonicalUrl,
105	      "Lightfast Blog",
106	      "Lightfast Blog"
107	    ),
108	    jsonLd: buildBlogPostJsonLd(data, url),
109	  };
110	}

> **Implementation deviation**: PostHog isn't wired in `apps/www` (only Vercel Analytics is). The `IntegrationCardAnalytics` wrapper below was **skipped entirely** during implementation — the index page uses a plain `<NavLink>` directly. Wire PostHog in a follow-up before reintroducing this component.

> **Implementation deviation — `comingSoon` access**: `PROVIDER_DISPLAY` is typed as a literal `as const` map, so TS narrows per-key — `comingSoon` only exists on apollo/linear/sentry, not github/vercel. Accessing `PROVIDER_DISPLAY[providerId].comingSoon` directly errors. Use:
> ```ts
> const providerComingSoon =
>   providerId && "comingSoon" in PROVIDER_DISPLAY[providerId]
>     ? PROVIDER_DISPLAY[providerId].comingSoon
>     : false;
> ```
> Phase 5's detail page derives the same `derivedStatus` and needs the same pattern.
